body:
### Control intent

This control supports the baseline requirement to **identities allowing external access**.

### Configuration reviewed

The assessment reviews the relevant account, subscription, project, tenant, or resource configuration in AWS and Google Cloud.

### Expected operation

Keep the setting owned, documented, and enforced through policy or infrastructure as code where possible. If an exception is required, record the owner, reason, compensating controls, and expiry date.
name: Identities allowing external access
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation
1. Edit role trust policy and remove broad principals.
2. Allow only explicit trusted role/account ARNs.
3. Add restrictive conditions for third-party use cases.

~~~bash
aws iam update-assume-role-policy --role-name <role-name> --policy-document file://trust-policy.json
~~~

## GCP remediation
1. Review service account IAM policy bindings.
2. Remove external members that are not approved.

~~~bash
gcloud iam service-accounts get-iam-policy <sa>@<project-id>.iam.gserviceaccount.com
gcloud iam service-accounts remove-iam-policy-binding <sa>@<project-id>.iam.gserviceaccount.com --member="user:<external-user@example.com>" --role="roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator"
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_update-role-trust-policy.html
- https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-account-permissions
